mappings.rationaleAI: Materials science covering fabrication processes, material properties of metals, ceramics, and polymers directly addresses materials processing (A), property modification (A.5), and physical metallurgy (B.3).
Engineering practice encompassing infrastructure construction and manufacturing relates to materials processing operations (A) and metallurgical processes (B).
Structural engineering covering beams, bridges, concrete, and timber structures connects to joining processes (A.4), welding principles and applications in bridges and ships (B.4, B.4.f).
Chemical processing principles including bonding and energy transfer underpin chemical processing of raw materials (A.1.b) and property modification through thermal and chemical means (A.5).
Thermodynamics, phase transitions, and activation energy connect to chemical processing (A.1.b) and thermal processing including heat-treating operations (A.5.a).
Chemical elements and their properties provide the foundational knowledge for extractive metallurgy (B.2) and physical metallurgy (B.3).
X-ray diffraction techniques for crystal structure determination connect to metallography (B.3.g) and inspection and testing methods (B.3.h).
Mineral families and crystallization processes connect to mineral processing of ores (B.1) and extractive metallurgy (B.2).

Iron production by Darby, textile machinery, and steam power innovations connect to processing solid materials through rolling and forging (A.2.b) and extractive metallurgy including pyrometallurgy (B.2).
Nanoscale science including self-assembly and molecular machinery connects to property modification of materials at atomic and molecular scales (A.5).
Phase transitions, symmetry breaking, and material properties in silicon chips and displays connect to property modification (A.5) and metallography (B.3.g).
Applications of radioactivity in industry including nuclear energy production connect to nuclear engineering metallurgy involving uranium and other nuclear metals (B.3.e).

Covers established applications of robotics in manufacturing and warehouse automation including sensing, actuation, and decision-making, directly relevant to materials-handling equipment and technology of storage and warehousing.

Covers innovation in industrial production including creative destruction, economies of scale, and disruptive technologies, relevant to types of materials-handling systems and transportation of materials in production processes.

Covers organizational concepts including supply chain dynamics, outsourcing, and information technology in production contexts, relevant to materials-handling systems by process and technology of storage and warehousing.
